document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report No. 269183
document.documentType: IEC 60601-1 safety test report with national deviations attachments
standards: Canadian Electrical Code (CEC), Part I, CSA C22.1

sourceSummary.oneLine: This chunk covers later IEC 60601-1 test tables for temperature, single fault, transformer, leakage-current, mechanical-strength topics, plus Canadian and Japanese national deviations for Report No. 269183.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory safety evidence for IEC 60601-1 compliance, end-product evaluation limitations, and national deviation review for Canada and Japan.
keyPoints: Model DEPS9601 passed excessive temperature testing at 90 V/60 Hz and 264 V/50 Hz with measured temperatures corrected to 40 °C.
Single fault short-circuit and overload tests for electrical components reported no hazardous situation, including several cases where the unit shut down immediately.
The power supply unit requires additional mechanical strength and end-product evaluation for some clauses.
Canadian national differences reference CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08 and several clauses are marked pass, not applicable, or to be evaluated in the end product.
Japanese national differences reference JIS T0601-1:2012 and identify JIS applicability requirements.

entities.name: DEPS9601
entities.evidence: Pages 127-128 identify 'Model No.: DEPS9601' for excessive temperature testing.

entities.name: Report No. 269183
entities.evidence: Multiple pages in the chunk state 'Report No. 269183'.

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Main report pages are headed 'IEC 60601-1' and use TRF No. IEC60601_1J.

entities.name: CSA International
entities.evidence: Canadian national deviations attachment lists 'Attachment Originator: CSA International'.

entities.name: TUV Rheinland Japan Ltd.
entities.evidence: Japan national differences attachment lists 'Attachment Originator: TUV Rheinland Japan Ltd.'.

entities.name: IECEE

concepts.name: Excessive temperatures in ME equipment
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.1.1 tables list thermocouple locations, allowable temperatures, measured temperatures, and verdict P.

concepts.name: Electrical single fault conditions
concepts.evidence: Clause 13.2.2 tables list short circuits and results with hazardous situation marked NO.

concepts.name: Impairment of cooling
concepts.evidence: Clause 13.2.7 describes all openings blocked at 90 V with transformer T1 coil stabilized at 94.5 °C and no hazard.

concepts.name: Transformer short circuit and overload testing
concepts.evidence: Clauses 15.5.1.2 and 15.5.1.3 are marked P and refer to appended table 13.2 results for switching transformer T1.

concepts.name: Canadian national deviations
concepts.evidence: Attachment identifies CA national differences to CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08.

concepts.name: Japan national differences
concepts.evidence: Attachment identifies Japan national differences according to JIS T0601-1:2012.

evidence.claim: Excessive temperature testing for model DEPS9601 passed at 90 V / 60 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 127: Clause 11.1.1, verdict P; Model No. DEPS9601; test ambient 23.8 °C; test supply 90 V / 60 Hz; listed measured temperatures corrected to 40 °C.

evidence.claim: Excessive temperature testing for model DEPS9601 passed at 264 V / 50 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 128: Clause 11.1.1, verdict P; Model No. DEPS9601; test ambient 23.9 °C; test supply 264 V / 50 Hz; listed measured temperatures corrected to 40 °C.

evidence.claim: A short circuit from +16 Vdc output to accessible metal enclosure produced immediate output shutdown and no hazardous situation.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 131: '+16 Vdc output to accessible metal enclosure'; input voltage 264 V; test time 10 min; 'All output shut downed immediately. No hazard.' hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: Several electrical single fault short-circuit tests under 264 V input resulted in no hazardous situation.
evidence.evidenceText: Pages 132-133 list short circuits for C13, PC2, PC1, C8, C5, U1, and T1 with hazardous situation marked NO.

evidence.claim: Short circuit of C5 caused F1 and F2 to open and U1 damage, but no hazardous situation was reported.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 133: Short circuit C5, input 264 V, test time 10 min; 'F1, F2 opened, U1 component damaged. No hazard.' hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: The +16 Vdc output overload test reported no hazard after 4 h 20 min.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 133: Clause 15.5.1.3 overload +16 Vdc o/p; input 264 V; test time 4 h 20 min; DC output overload 3.60 A; T1 winding stabilized at 83.1 °C; no hazard.

evidence.claim: No thermostats or temperature limiting devices were present in the equipment for relevant single fault clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 134: Clause 13.2.4 states 'No thermostats in the equipment'; clause 13.2.5 states 'No temperature limiting device.'

evidence.claim: Cooling impairment testing with all openings blocked resulted in no hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 134: all openings blocked; input voltage 90 V; input current 1.07 A; test time 1 h 4 min; transformer T1 coil 94.5 °C, ambient 23.4 °C; no hazard; hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: Mechanical strength tests were not fully evaluated for this power supply unit and require end-product evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 136: Clause 15.3 Mechanical Strength tests marked N/A; supplementary information says 'The equipment is a power supply unit. To be additionally evaluated in the end product.'

evidence.claim: Transformer short circuit and overload table sections are marked pass and refer to appended table 13.2 for switching transformer T1 results.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 137: Clauses 15.5.1.2 and 15.5.1.3 are marked P; notes state 'See appended table 13.2 of which results are for switching transformers, T1.'

evidence.claim: The Canadian national deviations attachment applies CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 1: 'CA - CANADIAN NATIONAL DIFFERENCES to CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08' and 'Differences according to: Canadian National standard: CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08.'

evidence.claim: Canadian component requirements under clause 4.8 were passed by reference to Table 8.10 in the main report.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 2: Clause 4.8 components; remarks 'See appended Table 8.10 in main test report No. 269183'; verdict P for items a and b.

evidence.claim: Canadian mains fuses and over-current releases requirement was passed by reference to Table 8.10.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 4: Clause 8.11.5; 'Mains fuses and OVER-CURRENT RELEASES shall be in accordance with the Canadian Electrical Code'; remark 'See appended Table 8.10 in main test report No. 269183'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Canadian internal wiring requirement was considered and passed by reference to Table 8.10.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 5: Clause 15.4.8; internal wiring shall be in accordance with CEC Part I, CSA C22.1; remark 'Considered, See appended Table 8.10 in main test report No. 269183'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The Japan national differences attachment applies JIS T0601-1:2012 as a modified adoption of IEC 60601-1:2005 Ed. 3.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 Page 6: 'JAPAN NATIONAL DIFFERENCES'; differences according to 'JIS T0601-1:2012 (IEC 60601-1:2005 Ed. 3 (MOD)).'

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 19 of 26; report cover information, full model scope, certificate identifiers, and final conclusions may appear outside this chunk.
Many tables in this chunk are marked N/A or refer to appended tables elsewhere, so unsupported detailed conclusions should not be inferred beyond the provided evidence.
